beg: verb. To request the help of someone, often in the form of money.
bel: noun. A measure of relative power, defined as log 10 (P 1 /P 2), where P 1 and P 2 are the measured and reference power respectively.

bilge: noun. The rounded portion of a ship 's hull, forming a transition between the bottom and the sides.
bill: noun. A written list or inventory. (Now obsolete except in specific senses or set phrases; bill of lading, bill of goods, etc.)
bluegill: noun. Any of the species Lepomis macrochirus of North American sunfish.
bug: noun. An insect of the order Hemiptera (the “true bugs”).
bugle: noun. A horn used by hunters.
bulge: noun. An object which is sticking out from a surface; a swelling, protuberant part; a bending outward, especially when caused by pressure.
bull: noun. An adult male, specifically: An adult male of domesticated cattle or oxen.
gel: noun. A semi-solid to almost solid colloid of a solid and a liquid, such as jelly, cheese or opal.
gib: noun. A castrated male cat or ferret.

gill: noun. A breathing organ of fish and other aquatic animals.
glib: adjective. Having a ready flow of words but lacking thought or understanding; superficial; shallow.
glue: noun. A hard gelatin made by boiling bones and hides, used in solution as an adhesive; or any sticky adhesive substance.
guile: noun. Astuteness often marked by a certain sense of cunning or artful deception.
gul: noun. Any medallionlike design on a Central or West Asian rug.
gull: noun. A seabird of the genus Larus or of the subfamily Larinae.
gullible: adjective. Easily deceived or duped; naive, easily cheated or fooled.
leg: noun. A limb or appendage that an animal uses for support or locomotion on land.
lei: noun. A garland of flowers in Hawaii.
leu: noun. The unit of currency of Romania, equal to one hundred bani.
libel: noun. A written or pictorial false statement which unjustly seeks to damage someone's reputation.
lie: verb. To rest in a horizontal position on a surface.
lug: noun. The act of hauling or dragging.

lull: noun. A period of rest or soothing.
